reasoning_model_1: The audio accurately recites the script 'I believe I can handle this job, I will give my best' with perfect content accuracy. The fluency is excellent, featuring a high-quality, natural-sounding female voice with no electronic artifacts. Regarding the core paralinguistic requirement (Confident tone), the model succeeds effectively. The delivery is paced deliberately (0:00-0:02), with a steady pitch and downward inflections at the end of clauses that typically signal certainty and assertiveness. The emphasis on 'handle' and 'best' reinforces a sense of capability. The tone is professional and assured, matching the 'Cognitive State' of confidence requested.
reasoning_model_2: The model recites the text accurately, but the performance fails in other dimensions. The audio quality is poor, sounding muffled, grainy, and distant, lacking the natural clarity of a modern TTS system. Crucially, regarding the core paralinguistic requirement, the audio completely fails to convey confidence. Instead, the voice sounds shaky, timid, and anxious, with a higher pitch and quavering delivery (0:00-0:04) that suggests fear or uncertainty rather than self-assurance. Since the model expresses the opposite of the requested tone (anxiety instead of confidence) and suffers from poor audio quality, it meets the criteria for the lowest score.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions perfectly: the content is accurate, the audio is high-fidelity and natural, and the tone is appropriately confident and assertive. Model 2, while getting the text right, failed significantly on the core task. The tone produced was anxious and insecure—the direct opposite of the requested 'confident tone'—and the audio quality was notably degraded (grainy/muffled). Model 1 is the clear winner as it successfully controlled the cognitive state feature.
